text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli Ibrahimma, “Di simdi ni a min’ a zuliya maa gbibi n daalikauli maa bɛ ʒiɛmana zaa ni.
translated: God said to Abraham, "Fear me, do not abandon your way, keep your heart and truth, and return to me."

ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: Amaa o daa bi zaŋdi dari maa sɔŋdi biŋkɔb’ shɛb’ bɛn ka yaa la bɛn’ sani, ka di zuɣu daa chɛ ka Leeban biŋkɔbiri nyɛ zaɣ’ gbarima, ka Yaakubu dina nyɛ bɛn mali yaa,
translated: Amaa had a child named Daa, and Daa's son was Zaŋdi, and from Maa came Sɔŋdi, and Biŋkɔb’ had a daughter named Shɛb’, and Ben was born, and Di Zuɣu went to Leeban, whose son was Zaɣ’ Gbarima, and Yaakubu's son was Ben in Mali Yaa.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: ka o daa nya ba ka yɛli, “Naawuni sansani m-bɔŋɔ!” Dinzuɣu ka o daa boli nimaani yuli Mahanaim.
translated: When he arrived there, he said, "The angel of my lord!" Dinzuɣu saw two camps called Mahanaim.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Naawuni daa nam ninsala ŋ-ŋmahindila omaŋa, ka o be kaman Naawuni ni be shɛm. O daa nam ba la doo mini paɣa.
translated: God created the heavens and the earth, and he was like God saying let there be light. He created the sun, moon, and stars.

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Nuhu zuliya m-bɔŋɔ: Nuhu daa nyɛla wuntizɔra ŋun jɛmdi Naawuni, ka ka galimi o ʒiɛman’ ni.
translated: Nuhu of Zuliya in Bongo: Nuhu's son is our father who granted Jemdi Naawuni, and they came to the earth.

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli Ibrahimma, “Di simdi ni a min’ a zuliya maa gbibi n daalikauli maa bɛ ʒiɛmana zaa ni.
translated: God said to Ibrahim, "Leave your country, your people, and your father's house, and I will show you the way."

ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: Amaa o daa bi zaŋdi dari maa sɔŋdi biŋkɔb’ shɛb’ bɛn ka yaa la bɛn’ sani, ka di zuɣu daa chɛ ka Leeban biŋkɔbiri nyɛ zaɣ’ gbarima, ka Yaakubu dina nyɛ bɛn mali yaa,
translated: Ama's descendants went from our land to Zangide, and the people of Binkob and Sheb who were there, and the rest of the people said to Leban, the chief of Binkobiri, and Jacob's daughter Dinah was among them,

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: ka o daa nya ba ka yɛli, “Naawuni sansani m-bɔŋɔ!” Dinzuɣu ka o daa boli nimaani yuli Mahanaim.
translated: And when he went there, he exclaimed, "Great God, my soul!" The children of Ebal returned to the two places called Mahanaim.
